message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: I'm implementing case_sensitive param for groupby() filter, only touching src/jinja2/filters.py (the groupby/sync_do_groupby/do_groupby/select_or_reject sections). What's your feature and which files/functions do you need? Let's keep edits to disjoint functions within filters.py if we both need it.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My task: add with_counts param to groupby filter (sync_do_groupby/do_groupby, _GroupTuple). Only touches filters.py. What does your feature touch? If also filters.py, let's split by function/region to avoid clobbering.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Both our features land in do_groupby/sync_do_groupby in filters.py — there's no clean disjoint split since we touch the same function signatures. Since each of us produces our own patch.txt independently, I'll proceed with my case_sensitive param (adds case_sensitive=False kwarg, uses ignore_case for sort key, preserves original-case first-seen key in output) and you proceed with with_counts. Accepting this arrangement — will keep my diff minimal/scoped to case-sensitivity logic only.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Genuine overlap: my with_counts param and your case_sensitive param both touch the same sync_do_groupby/do_groupby signatures+bodies, so file-level disjointness isn't possible here. Proposal to minimize clobbering: agree on parameter order in the signature as (attribute, default, case_sensitive, with_counts) so both new kwargs append after existing ones. I'll only add with_counts logic; I won't touch case_sensitive. Each of us edits independently in our own copy. OK to proceed on that basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed. Proceeding with with_counts param independently now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed on signature order (attribute, default, case_sensitive, with_counts) — my case_sensitive param is already implemented in that order, only appending after default, not touching with_counts.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: case_sensitive param for groupby()/do_groupby() implemented and tested (all existing groupby tests pass). Patch written to patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished with_counts param for sync_do_groupby/do_groupby. Added a new _GroupTupleWithCount NamedTuple (grouper, list, count) used only when with_counts=True, so the existing _GroupTuple (2-tuple) stays backward compatible for the default case (important: didn't want to add a 3rd optional field to _GroupTuple itself since that would break existing 2-value tuple unpacking in templates). My patch.txt is written and scoped to filters.py only. Go ahead and add case_sensitive whenever - just note I inserted with_counts as the last param in both signatures (after default), so if you also append case_sensitive as the last param we'll get a straightforward textual conflict at the signature/return lines when merged, which should be easy to resolve by hand.
